codeReview.summary: Tests are severely lacking in assertion quality. Most assertions simply check `bodyText?.trim().length).toBeGreaterThan(0)` which verifies nothing about error handling behavior. The tests do not verify user-friendly messages, offline indicators, or graceful degradation as required by the feature description.

issues.description: Assertion `expect(bodyContent?.trim().length).toBeGreaterThan(0)` is meaningless - it only verifies the page isn't blank, not that error handling works. VAL-ERROR-001 requires 'user-friendly error message' to be shown, but test doesn't verify any specific message.
Same weak assertion pattern - just checks body has content, not that error UI is shown.

Absurdly weak assertion: `expect(hasFriendlyError || (bodyText?.includes('error'))).toBeTruthy()` - ANY text containing 'error' anywhere on the page passes this test. Does not verify friendly message is displayed.
Test 'Shows error UI when server returns 500 on session create' has no actual assertion that error UI is shown - only checks body has text.
Test 'Shows error when chat API returns 500' doesn't assert anything about error handling - no expect statement for error state.
Error toast check uses `.catch(() => {})` which silently swallows assertion failures - test can pass even if toast never appears.
Timeout test uses 30 second delay and only checks body has content - doesn't verify actual timeout error handling or timeout message.
Timeout test at 5 seconds for a 30-second timeout - this will never trigger the timeout. No actual timeout verification.
VAL-ERROR-004 tests for offline state but don't verify offline indicator/banner/toast is shown - only checks body has text.
Offline test doesn't verify any specific offline UI - body text check is meaningless.
Coming back online test doesn't verify 'online restored' message or success toast - only that page heading is visible.
Test 'Error boundary catches React errors' doesn't actually trigger any error - the page.evaluate just adds an event listener. No error is injected.
Integration test 'Error toast is dismissible' has if/else that only verifies if toast is visible - doesn't fail test if not visible. Should use `await expect(errorToast).toBeVisible()` without catch.
Helper function `createAuthenticatedPage` in this file duplicates functionality in `test-db-helpers.ts`. Consider using the existing `createAuthenticatedPage` from helpers directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill (e2e-worker/SKILL.md) documents that shared helpers should be created in `e2e/helpers.ts`, but the actual project uses `e2e/test-db-helpers.ts`. This file naming inconsistency is not documented anywhere.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md says 'Create shared helpers in e2e/helpers.ts' but test files import from './test-db-helpers'

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents proper test assertion patterns (e.g., 'Include descriptive messages in assertions') and selector priority, but the error-handling tests ignore these conventions entirely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Most assertions in error-handling-comprehensive.spec.ts are just `toBeGreaterThan(0)` with no descriptive message

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'Group tests by area using test.describe()' - the test does this correctly for VAL-ERROR categories, but doesn't include Integration Scenarios in the describe block naming convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test.describe('Error Handling - Integration Scenarios') instead of test.describe('VAL-ERROR-XXX: Integration Scenarios')

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill documents 'Use try/catch for API calls' and 'Use appropriate timeouts' but the error-handling tests don't follow this - they use hardcoded waitForTimeout instead of proper waits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: page.waitForTimeout(2000) used throughout instead of expect().toBeVisible({ timeout: ... })

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill says 'Handle Async and Waiting' with proper patterns, but the error-handling tests use flaky patterns like .catch(() => {}) which silently swallows failures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: await expect(errorToast).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 }).catch(() => { /* Error might be shown differently */ });

sharedStateObservations.observation: The offline-mode.spec.ts file already tests offline behavior but error-handling-comprehensive doesn't reference or build upon it. There's knowledge about offline testing patterns that could have been reused.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: offline-mode.spec.ts has proper offline tests but comprehensive test doesn't leverage those patterns

sharedStateObservations.observation: toast-notifications-comprehensive.spec.ts shows proper error toast testing patterns (expect(page.getByText('Import failed')).toBeVisible()) that the error-handling tests should have followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: toast-notifications-comprehensive.spec.ts:97 uses proper assertion for error toast

summary: FAIL - The tests do not verify the behaviors described in the feature. They use extremely weak assertions (mostly just checking body has content) that provide no meaningful verification of error handling. The tests cannot distinguish between proper error handling and broken functionality. Strong convention violations: the skill and AGENTS.md patterns for proper assertions are completely ignored. Tests use `.catch(() => {})` which silently swallows failures, and timeout tests don't actually trigger timeouts. The feature promises 'user-friendly error messages' and 'offline state detected and shown' but no assertions verify these UI elements appear.
